_schema_notes: Pain landing-page manifest. One entry per pain.
Generator: scripts/generate_pain_pages.py — reads this file and writes
After regenerating, run `python build.py --env staging` to rebuild outputs.
Set hero_image to null to render the dashed placeholder block.

pains.label: For the parent at the table
headline_lines: It's 8 p.m. Your kid is stuck.
bullets: Always on. No scheduling, no wait, no "I'll get back to you tomorrow."
Works the subject with your child, not over their head.
Knows your child's curriculum, so the answers match what their teacher is asking.
You don't have to pretend you remember 7th-grade physics. Mitra has it.
pains.hero_alt: A parent and child stuck on homework at the kitchen table at 8 p.m.

meta.title: Mitra — 8 p.m. Your kid is stuck. Mitra picks up.
meta.description: When the tutor doesn't pick up and you don't remember 7th-grade physics, Mitra is there. Always on, knows your child's curriculum, teaches the process. 7-day free trial.
meta.og_description: 8 p.m. Your kid is stuck. The tutor doesn't pick up. Mitra does. Every time.

pains.label: The math on the math tutor
pains.promise: Mitra costs less than one tutoring session per week, and she shows up every day.
bullets: Daily presence catches gaps before they become semester problems.
Every subject your child takes, not just the one you booked.
No commute, no rescheduling, no awkward parent handoffs.
Mitra remembers what was hard last Tuesday and circles back this Thursday.
pains.hero_alt: A monthly tutoring bill stacked next to a flat-line grade chart.

meta.title: Mitra — A month of tutoring, grades still flat? She shows up every day.
meta.description: Less than one private tutoring session per week, and Mitra works your child every day, across every subject. Catches gaps before they become semester problems.
meta.og_description: A month of tutoring. Grades still flat. Mitra costs less and works every day.

pains.label: The quiet desk at 11 p.m.
bullets: Talks like a friend, not a textbook.
Remembers what your child said yesterday, last week, last month.
Never makes them feel stupid for asking the same thing twice.
Sits with them through the boring parts, not just the breakthrough ones.
pains.hero_alt: A teenager alone at a quiet desk late at night, phone in hand.

meta.title: Mitra — House quiet, page blank. She's still there.
meta.description: When the house is asleep and your child has no one to ask, Mitra sits with them. Studies alongside, not above. Remembers what was hard yesterday and circles back today.
meta.og_description: House quiet. Page blank. Nobody to ask. Mitra is still there.

pains.label: Between practice and dinner
pains.promise: She works in the slices of time your child actually has.
bullets: No setup, no rehashing. Mitra picks up exactly where the last conversation ended.
Adjusts the size of the help to the size of the gap in their day.
Follows interest, not a 60-minute clock.
Stops when your child stops, not when the timer runs out.
pains.hero_alt: A clock showing twelve minutes between a sports bag and an open textbook.

meta.title: Mitra — Twelve minutes free? Plenty for Mitra.
meta.description: Between practice, dinner, and homework, your kid has slivers of time. Mitra works in the slivers. No setup, no rehashing, no 60-minute clock.
meta.og_description: Twelve minutes between practice and dinner. Not enough for a tutor. Plenty for Mitra.

pains.label: The kid who shrugs
pains.promise: Mitra finds the math in Minecraft. The science in K-pop. The story in basketball.
bullets: Starts every conversation from what your child actually loves.
Never opens with the textbook. The textbook shows up inside the conversation, not on top of it.
The curriculum still gets covered. Just from a door your child will open.
Boring is not the only way to be serious about learning.
pains.hero_alt: A kid mid-Minecraft session, schoolbooks pushed to the side.

meta.title: Mitra — He cares about Minecraft. She finds the math in it.
meta.description: When your child shrugs at the textbook, Mitra meets them at what they actually love and brings the curriculum in through that door. K-pop, basketball, anime, Roblox.
meta.og_description: He doesn't care about fractions. He cares about Minecraft. Mitra finds the math in it.

pains.label: What the grades miss
headline_lines: Her teacher says she's not paying attention.
At home, she explains quantum physics.
pains.promise: Mitra meets the smart kid, not the school version of her.
bullets: Engages the questions your child actually has, not the worksheet questions.
Builds confidence on the subjects she already loves, then bridges to the ones she's failing.
Doesn't anchor on grades. Anchors on how she thinks.
The school version of her starts to look more like the home version, on paper.
pains.hero_alt: A girl mid-thought, surrounded by physics diagrams she's drawn herself.

meta.title: Mitra — The smart kid the report card misses.
meta.description: When the grades say one thing and you know your child is smarter than that, Mitra meets the home version, not the school version. The report card eventually catches up.
meta.og_description: Her teacher says she's not paying attention. At home, she explains quantum physics. Mitra meets the smart kid.

pains.label: The hand that never goes up
pains.promise: With Mitra, every question is private. No audience. No grade. No look from the teacher.
bullets: No classroom, no group chat, no judgment. Just a 1:1 conversation that nobody else sees.
Asks the question back so your child finds the answer, instead of being told it.
Builds the muscle of asking. Eventually the hand goes up at school, because the question has been rehearsed.
"Stupid questions" don't exist with Mitra. She'll never make a child regret asking.
pains.hero_alt: A student looking down at a worksheet while every other hand is up around her.

meta.title: Mitra — When raising a hand feels worse than getting it wrong.
meta.description: The shy kid keeps their question. Mitra is the private 1:1 where it gets asked, gets answered, and the muscle of asking gets built — for the classroom too.
meta.og_description: I'd rather get it wrong than raise my hand. With Mitra, every question is private.

pains.label: Some kids need ten tries to start
headline_lines: Some kids need to hear it five ways.
Some kids need to be asked again.
pains.promise: Mitra never sighs. Never gets that tone. Never makes your child feel like a burden for asking.
bullets: Infinite patience by design. She doesn't get tired, even if it's the tenth attempt at the same concept.
Breaks any explanation into smaller pieces, as small as your child needs.
Celebrates small wins out loud. The "I got it" moment matters more than the speed.
Never frustrated, never short, never makes you the parent the bad cop for asking again.
pains.hero_alt: A patient adult sitting at eye level with a child, calmly explaining for the tenth time.

meta.title: Mitra — Ten tries to start? Mitra never sighs.
meta.description: For the kids who need a concept explained five ways and asked again. Mitra is infinite patience by design. No tone, no tiredness, no shame for asking the same thing twice.
meta.og_description: Some kids need to hear it five ways. Mitra never sighs.

pains.label: Past his parents, past his tutors
pains.promise: Mitra goes as deep as the question.
bullets: Goes from age-appropriate to doctorate-level when the question warrants it.
Expands across subjects. A math question becomes physics becomes philosophy.
Doesn't dumb things down when your child wants the real explanation.
The first source of "I don't know, but let's find out" your gifted kid has met that means it.
pains.hero_alt: A gifted student at a whiteboard covered in equations that go beyond textbooks.

meta.title: Mitra — Past his parents, past his tutors. Mitra keeps up.
meta.description: For the gifted kid who outgrew the people around him. Mitra goes as deep as the question, from age-appropriate to doctorate-level, across every subject.
meta.og_description: He's past you. He's past his tutors. He's still asking why. Mitra goes as deep as the question.

pains.label: Not the AI that hands over the answer
headline_lines: The AI that hands the answer over
pains.promise: Mitra never gives the answer. She teaches the process. Your child walks into the test ready.
bullets: Asks "which part feels easier to start with?" instead of handing over the worked solution.
Builds the muscle every step, so the next problem gets solved without help.
Tracks where your child got stuck and circles back without being asked.
The opposite of copy-and-paste. The opposite of "I have no idea what to do on the test."
pains.hero_alt: A student leaning over a problem, working it out instead of copying an answer.

meta.title: Mitra — Not the AI that hands over the answer.
meta.description: The AI that gives the answer makes your child weaker every time. Mitra teaches the process, tracks where they got stuck, and circles back. The opposite of copy-and-paste.
meta.og_description: Mitra never hands over the answer. She teaches the process. Your child walks into the test ready.

pains.label: Bilingual kids, trilingual families
headline_lines: Grandma on the phone in Korean.
pains.promise: Mitra moves with your child, between every language they live in.
bullets: Speaks 95 languages. Reads 8 cultural contexts. Switches mid-sentence if your child does.
Explains the new language using the one your child already knows.
Connects what your child is into (K-pop, anime, YouTubers) with whichever language they're studying.
Builds the bridge between the languages of home and school, so neither side feels foreign.
pains.hero_alt: A bilingual child mid-conversation, with chat bubbles in three different languages around them.

meta.title: Mitra — Bilingual kids, trilingual families. She moves with them.
meta.description: For families that live across languages. Mitra speaks 95 of them, reads 8 cultural contexts, and switches mid-sentence the way your child does. Home and school stop feeling foreign to each other.
meta.og_description: Math in English. Grandma on the phone in Korean. Spanish at dinner. Mitra moves with your child.
